title: ulmus glabra f. purpurea
intro: Ulmus glabra f. purpurea, also known as the 'Purpurea' or 'Sorbus' of the Wych Elm, is a type of deciduous tree that belongs to the Ulmaceae family. Traditionally, the bark of this tree has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ulmus glabra f. purpurea include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
